reasoning: World records in long-course swimming are rarely broken between November and February, as this period corresponds to the short-course (25m pool) season and heavy training cycles, culminating in the World Short Course Championships in December. Lilly King has held the 100m breaststroke long-course record (1:04.13) since 2017. While Tang Qianting swam a 1:04.39 in April 2024, she is currently competing in the short-course circuit (having recently broken Asian records in the 25m format). Given the lack of major long-course taper meets before February 2026, the probability of the record falling is extremely low. Uncertainty slightly increases for the furthest date as some early-season long-course meets (e.g., TYR Pro Swim Series) occur in early 2026.
